input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on Windows 11 (Android + Windows targets):

---

## Step 1 — Install the .NET SDK

```powershell
# Download and run the official installer
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9
# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## Step 2 — Install the MAUI workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to confirm the name
dotnet workload search maui

# Install MAUI (includes Android, Windows SDKs)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## Step 3 — Install Java (OpenJDK)

Android builds require JDK 17. The MAUI workload may bundle one, but to be explicit:

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.OpenJDK.17

# Verify
java -version

# Ensure JAVA_HOME is set (winget usually does this; if not):
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable(
  "JAVA_HOME",
  (Get-Command java).Source | Split-Path | Split-Path,
  "User"
)
```

## Step 4 — Android SDK setup

The MAUI workload auto-installs the Android SDK. Discover what's installed and what's needed:

```powershell
# Find where the Android SDK landed
$androidSdk = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"
if (!(Test-Path $androidSdk)) {
  # Alternate location used by VS workloads
  $androidSdk = "$env:ProgramFiles(x86)\Android\android-sdk"
}
Write-Host "Android SDK at: $androidSdk"

# List installed platforms & build-tools
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list_installed

# Accept all licenses (required for builds)
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses

# If you need a specific API level (discover latest available):
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list |
  Select-String "platforms;android-"

# Install a specific API (e.g., 35)
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" "platforms;android-35" "build-tools;35.0.0"

# Set ANDROID_HOME if not already present
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", $androidSdk, "User")
```

## Step 5 — Android emulator (optional but recommended)

```powershell
# Install emulator + a system image
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "emulator" `
  "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64"

# Create an AVD
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
  -n "Pixel_MAUI" `
  -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" `
  --device "pixel_6"

# Verify Hyper-V / WHPX is enabled (required for emulator perf)
Get-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V-All |
  Select-Object State
# Enable if needed (requires reboot)
Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V-All
```

## Step 6 — Enable Windows App SDK target

```powershell
# Windows targets need Developer Mode
Start-Process "ms-settings:developers"
# Then toggle "Developer Mode" ON in the UI

# Verify Windows App SDK is present (bundled with MAUI workload)
dotnet workload list | Select-String "maui-windows"
```

## Step 7 — Create and build a test project

```powershell
# Discover available MAUI templates
dotnet new list maui

# Create a new project
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for Windows (WinUI 3)
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Android emulator
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Run
```

## Step 8 — Dynamically discover target framework versions

If you're unsure of exact TFM versions:

```powershell
# See what TFMs your SDK supports
dotnet --info | Select-String "Microsoft.Android|Microsoft.Maui|Microsoft.Windows"

# Inspect the generated .csproj for default TFMs
Get-Content HelloMaui.csproj | Select-String "TargetFrameworks"

# List available Android API levels in your SDK
dotnet workload list
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list_installed |
  Select-String "platforms;android-"
```

---

**Summary of tools installed:**

| Component | Source |
|---|---|
| .NET 9 SDK | `winget` |
| MAUI workload | `dotnet workload install` |
| OpenJDK 17 | `winget` |
| Android SDK/tools | Bundled with MAUI workload |
| Hyper-V | Windows optional feature |

Run the steps in order. The `dotnet workload install maui` step does the heaviest lifting — it pulls in Android SDK, build tools, and Windows App SDK automatically. The remaining steps verify and fill gaps.
data.reasoningText: The user wants a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 targeting Android and Windows. They can't run commands now, so they want all the PowerShell commands laid out. Let me provide a comprehensive guide.
